title: A system and method for obtaining 3-dimensional images using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images
abstract: A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2-dimensional x-ray image, said method comprising: acquiring at least an x-ray image of a bone of interest; determining camera model to at least determine spatial values of source and at least one spatial value of bone; extracting a contour and distinct anatomical regions; identifying anatomical values in terms of 2-dimensional anatomical values; importing a pre-created 3-dimensional template; extracting silhouette vertices of said pre-created 3-dimensional template and their projections; aligning said pre-created 3-dimensional template wrt said input x-ray image; projecting said pre-created 3-dimensional template onto said acquired image plane to obtain a 2-dimensional projection model; modifying said aligned template to match said identified 2-dimensional values; determining a best matching point on said extracted contour; back-projecting each of said best matching; determining a target position; and deforming said pre-created 3-dimensional template in order to obtain a 3-dimensional reconstructed image.
claims:
Claims Claims, 1. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said method comprising the steps of: - acquiring at least an x-ray image of a bone of interest; - determining camera model, of said acquired at least an x-ray image, using known parameters to at least determine spatial values of source and to determine at least one spatial value of bone; - extracting a contour of said bone from said image, said contour comprising distinct anatomical regions; - identifying anatomical values in terms of 2-dimensional anatomical values from said extracted distinct anatomical regions of said contour; - importing a pre-created 3-dimensional template corresponding to said bone along with pre-determined anatomical values and pre-determined anatomical values; - extracting silhouette vertices of said pre-created 3-dimensional template and their projections, for each silhouette vertex projection, according to said camera model and default initial alignment of said pre-created 3-dimensional template; - aligning said pre-created 3-dimensional template of said bone with respect to said input x-ray image using a pre-determined method; - projecting said pre-created 3-dimensional template on to said acquired image plane, using said camera model, to obtain a 2-dimensional projection model; - modifying said aligned template to match said identified 2-dimensional values using at least an anatomical value from said image to 2-dimensional projection of corresponding identified anatomical values of said pre-created 3-dimensional template; - determining a best matching point on said extracted contour, for each of said extracted silhouette vertex projection, for 2-dimensional to 2-dimensional correspondence, of silhouette vertex projection and said extracted contour; - back-projecting each of said best matching points according to said camera model to form a back projected ray, said ray being formed by said source and said best matching point; - determining a target position, said target position being a position, on each of said back projected rays, that is closest to a corresponding silhouette vertex; and 

 - deforming said pre-created 3-dimensional template such that said extracted silhouette vertices achieve positions of their corresponding target positions in order to obtain a 3- dimensional reconstructed image. 2. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein said step of determining a best matching point using self-organising maps' method. 3. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein said step of deforming using Laplacian Mesh Deformation method. 4. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein said camera model comprising spatial parameters and spatial values relating to at least a source, at least an object, at least an image, and at least a plane. 5. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein said anatomical values being selected from a set consisting of spatial values per landmark, correlation between spatial values per landmark, and parameters defined by spatial values per landmark. 6. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said x-ray image comprises no markers and no orientation data of said bone with respect to image. 7. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model using known parameters comprising a step of determining position of source of x-ray and said image with respect to each other. 8. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model using known parameters comprising a step of determining magnification factor of said x-ray image. 9. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of extracting a contour comprising a step of determining and digitizing spatial values of contours of said bone of said image. 10. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model using known parameters comprising a step of determining camera model using at least position of x-ray source with respect to said x-ray image. 11. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of identifying pre-determined anatomical values in terms of 2-dimensional values from said extracted contour comprising a step of determining length values and angle values. 12. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model comprising steps of: - determining image plane in 3D imaging space that corresponds to a detector plane, a plane coinciding with flat x-ray sensor panel or a film of a real imaging environment, where projection of a bone is formed; - determining image center as a central position of a rectangular detector; - determining position of X-ray source with respect to said image plane, in imaging space; - determining distance between object's (bone) centroid and the X-ray source, measured in the direction normal to the image plane, in the imaging space. 13. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model comprising steps of: - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; 

 - determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; - determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance. 14. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model comprising steps of - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; - determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; - determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; - determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance; - said source object distance being determined by steps comprising further steps of: - placing a spherical ball marker with a known actual diameter near the object (bone/body) during X-ray imaging, closer to the image center, at a height from the detector plane, that is closer to the height of the object's centroid from the detector plane, by eyeballing. computing source object distance as multiplication of source film distance and the ratio of the known actual diameter of the spherical ball marker to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane; - determining diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane' as equal to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker measured on the final X-ray image multiplied by a digital magnification ratio, said digital magnification ratio being a ratio of the value of the 

 distance between the positions of projections of any two points on the object's (bone) surface on the detector plane to the value of the distance between the corresponding points as measured in the final X-ray image. 15. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining camera model comprising steps of - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; - determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; - determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; - determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance; - said source object distance being determined by steps comprising further steps of: placing a spherical ball marker with a known actual diameter near the object (bone/body) during X-ray imaging, closer to the image center, at a height from the detector plane, that is closer to the height of the object's centroid from the detector plane, by eyeballing. - computing source object distance as multiplication of source film distance and the ratio of the known actual diameter of the spherical ball marker to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane; - determining diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane' as equal to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker measured on the final X-ray image multiplied by a digital magnification ratio, said digital magnification ratio being determined by a circular coin marker with known actual diameter placed on the detector while taking the X-ray image, said digital magnification ratio being approximately equal to the ratio of the known actual diameter of the circular coin to diameter of the coin as visible on the final X-ray image, as measured in terms of number of pixels or mm. 16. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising the steps of: - determination of anatomical regions, said anatomical values being 2-dimensional positions of unique anatomical features identified on the final X-ray image on the basis anatomical regions; and - determination of anatomical values based on determined anatomical regions, said anatomical parameters being values of geometric parameters calculated on the basis of anatomical landmarks for use in 3-dimensional reconstruction. 17. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising the steps of: - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said manual method comprising further step of specifying two points on the image that lie on the axis. 18. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising the steps of: - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; - determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said manual method comprising further step of determining a given line along a position and orientation of the required axis. 19. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising the steps of: 

 - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; - determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said automatic method comprising further steps of: performing geometric calculation on distinguished anatomical regions of the contour; - determining positions of a few anatomical values on the final X-ray image with respect to the extracted contours on the basis of anatomical regions. 20. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising a step of calculating anatomical parameters on the basis of anatomical landmarks wherein parameters can be distance between two landmarks, angle between lines defined by any two landmarks, or any correlative value between landmarks. 21. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of determining anatomical values comprising a step of determining anatomical regions, axes, anatomical landmarks, and anatomical parameters of said pre-created 3D template model, said anatomical regions corresponding to regions distinguished in the contour of said X-ray image, said anatomical landmarks identified on the basis of anatomical regions of said template being same as the anatomical landmarks identified on the basis of anatomical regions of said contour, said anatomical parameters identified on the basis of anatomical landmarks of said template being same as the anatomical parameters identified on the basis of anatomical landmarks of the contour. 22. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of importing a pre-created 3-dimensional template comprising further steps of: - providing a corresponding bone template model in 3-dimensional format, said corresponding bone template model format belonging to a clinically normal bone in the form of 3D mesh with triangular elements, said bone template model being reconfigured into a shape that matches the input contours. 23. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said pre-created 3-dimensional template is formed in the form of mesh, pre-created from CT scan of a healthy subject with average weight and height; such a data set with multiple subjects being created and wherein demographics and gender of subjects being used to discretise the data set. 24. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said pre-created 3-dimensional template is formed in the form of mesh, pre-created from CT scan of a healthy subject with average weight and height wherein by segmentation of all the slices images of CT scan, a 3D surface model is created. 25. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said pre-created 3-dimensional template is formed in the form of mesh, pre-created from CT scan of a healthy subject with average weight and height wherein by segmentation of all the slices images of CT scan, a 3D surface model is created, said surface model being exported as point cloud surface model, connectivity between said points of said point cloud being formed using constrained Delaunay Triangulation method to form a 3D mesh model with triangular elements, said triangular element being an element defined by forming connectivity between three points, wherein by triangulation of all points of said point cloud, a mesh of triangular element is formed, said point cloud being sampled to reduce the number of surface points, and hence the number of triangular elements resulting from meshing. 26. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of extracting silhouette vertices comprising a step of determining those vertices of said pre-created 3-dimensional template which form the outer contour of the template's projection on the image plane, according to said camera model, hereinafter called as 'template projection contour'. 27. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 1 wherein, said step of extracting silhouette vertices comprising a step of determining those vertices of said pre-created 3-dimensional template which form the outer contour of the template's projection on the image plane, according to said camera model, hereinafter called as 'template projection contour', said template projection contour being computed using the steps of: - projecting all the vertices of the template on the image plane (perspective projection); - obtaining triangulation meshing of projection using Delaunay triangulation method; creating a 2-dimensional mesh with triangular elements using constraint Delaunay triangulation method from the projected points; and - identifying edges of the triangular elements which are shared with only one triangular element, said identified edges being boundary edges and the corresponding projected points being boundary point and hence the template projection contour points. 28. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said method comprising further steps of: acquiring at least a first x-ray image of a bone of interest in a first known view; - performing 3-dimensional reconstruction with respect to a first known view using said method of claim 1 in order to obtain a first 3-dimensional reconstructed image; - acquiring at least a second x-ray image of a bone of interest in a second known view; and - using said obtained first 3-dimensional reconstructed image as a pre-created template and performing 3-dimensional reconstruction with respect to a second known view using said method of claim 1 in order to obtain a second 3-dimensional reconstructed image. 29. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said method comprising further steps of: obtaining a first set of target positions for a first set of silhouette vertices extracted from said pre-created 3-dimensional template which is aligned with respect to a first known view using method steps of claim 1 ; - obtaining a second set of target positions for a second set of silhouette vertices extracted from said pre-created 3-dimensional template which is aligned with respect to a second known view using method steps of claim 1 ; and 

 - deforming said pre-created 3-dimensional template such that said position of said first set of silhouette vertices matches said first set of target positions and such that said position of said second set of silhouette vertices matches said second set of target positions. 30. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said method comprising further steps as claimed in claim 1 using Laplacian Mesh Deformation method. 31. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said first known view and said second known view are view orthogonal to each other. 32. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said first known view and said second known view are view orthogonal to each other and wherein a combined camera model is determined based on individual camera models of the x-ray images of the said two views, wherein further the template position with respect to the two images is found by matching the two template projection contours calculated based on the two images according to the two camera models using shape registration method. 33. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said first known view and said second known view are views orthogonal to each other and wherein said step of identifying anatomical landmarks comprising a further step of calculating 3-dimensional values of said anatomical values using said 2-dimensional values from said first x-ray image and from said second x-ray image. 34. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said first known view and said second known view are views orthogonal to each other and wherein said step of modifying said aligned template to match said identified 2-dimensional values comprising a further step of matching 3- dimensional values of corresponding identified anatomical parameters of said pre-created 

 template to said identified 3-dimensional values of anatomical parameters from both image. 35. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said first known view and said second known view are views orthogonal to each other and wherein said step of identifying anatomical landmarks comprising a further step of calculating 3-dimensional values of said anatomical values using said 2-dimensional values from said first x-ray image and from said second x-ray image and wherein said step of modifying comprising a further step of modifying of said aligned pre-created 3-dimensional template to match said calculated 3-dimensional values to identified anatomical values of said pre-created 3-dimensional template. 36. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said step of aligning said pre-created 3-dimensional template of said bone with respect to said at least an input x-ray image using a pre-determined method, said pre-determined method comprising the steps of: - mapping said pre-created 3-dimensional template with said image in terms of its view; extracting at least two position values of identifiable landmarks from said x-ray image; - extracting corresponding landmark vertices from said corresponding pre-created 3- dimensional template; - calculating projections of said extracted landmark vertices on an input x-ray image plane obtained from said camera model; - calculating contour ratio of inter-landmark distance, from said extracted contour, along a direction with respect to at least one anatomical axis of said bone; - calculating template projection ratio of inter-landmark distance, from said projection of template, along a direction with respect to the said anatomical axis of said bone; - angularly displacing said template about an axis which is orthogonal to said anatomical axis in a way that said template projection ratio matches said input contour ratio, in order to define angular displacement requirement; - calculating contour of said template projection contour; and - angularly displacing said pre-created 3-dimensional template about an axis normal to said image plane to obtain a best fit of said template projection contour to said input contour. 37. A method as claimed in claim 27 wherein said step of calculating contour of said projection of said template using triangulation method. 38. A method as claimed in claim 27 wherein said step of angularly displacing said pre- created 3-dimensional template about an axis normal to said image plane to obtain a best fit of said projection contour to said input contour using shape registration method. 39. A method for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said step of mapping said pre-created 3-dimensional template with said image in terms of its view further comprising the steps of: - angularly displacing said bone template about an axis which is normal to said image plane, such that any anatomical axis calculated for said projection contour matches principal axes for said input contour; - translating said template such that centroid of said projection contour matches centroid of said input contour; and - truncating said template to match aspect ratio of its projection with that of said contour extracted from said image, if contour is not complete. 40. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using at least one conventional 2- dimensional x-ray image, said system comprising: - data import mechanism configured to acquire at least an x-ray image of a bone of interest; - camera model determination mechanism configured to determine camera model, of said acquired at least an x-ray image, using known parameters to at least determine spatial values of source and to determine at least one spatial value of bone; - contouring mechanism configured to extract a contour of said bone from said image, said contour comprising distinct anatomical regions; identifying anatomical values in terms of 2-dimensional anatomical values from said extracted distinct anatomical regions of said contour; 

 - bone template model input mechanism configured to import a pre-created 3-dimensional template corresponding to said bone along with pre-determined anatomical values and pre-determined anatomical values; - silhouette vertices extraction mechanism configured to extract silhouette vertices of said pre-created 3-dimensional template and their projections, for each silhouette vertex projection, according to said camera model and default initial alignment of said pre- created 3-dimensional template; - alignment mechanism configured to align said pre-created 3-dimensional template of said bone with respect to said input x-ray image using a pre-determined method; - projection mechanism configured to project said pre-created 3-dimensional template on to said acquired image plane, using said camera model, to obtain a 2-dimensional projection model; - selective anatomical modification mechanism configured to modify said aligned template to match said identified 2-dimensional values using at least an anatomical value from said image to 2-dimensional projection of corresponding identified anatomical values of said pre-created 3-dimensional template; - matching point mechanism configured to determine a best matching point on said extracted contour, for each of said extracted silhouette vertex projection, for 2- dimensional to 2-dimensional correspondence, of silhouette vertex projection and said extracted contour; - back-projecting each of said best matching points according to said camera model to form a back projected ray, said ray being formed by said source and said best matching point; determining a target position, said target position being a position, on each of said back projected rays, that is closest to a corresponding silhouette vertex; and - template deformation mechanism configured to deform said pre-created 3-dimensional template such that said extracted silhouette vertices achieve positions of their corresponding target positions in order to obtain a 3-dimensional reconstructed image. 41. A system as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said matching point mechanism being configured to perform a step of determining a best matching point using self-organising maps' method. 42. A system as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said template deformation mechanism being configured to perform a step of deforming using Laplacian Mesh Deformation method. 43. A system as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism camera model comprising spatial parameters and spatial values relating to at least a source, at least an object, at least an image, and at least a plane. 44. A system as claimed in claim 40, wherein said anatomical values being selected from a set consisting of spatial values per landmark, correlation between spatial values per landmark, and parameters defined by spatial values per landmark. 45. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said x-ray image comprises no markers and no orientation data of said bone with respect to image. 46. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model using known parameters, being characterised to perform a step of determining position of source of x-ray and said image with respect to each other. 47. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model using known parameters, being characterised to perform a step of determining magnification factor of said x-ray image. 48. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for extracting a contour, being characterised to perform a step of determining and digitizing spatial values of contours of said bone of said image. 49. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model using known parameters, being characterised to perform a step of determining camera model using at least position of x-ray source with respect to said x-ray image. 50. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for identifying predetermined anatomical values in terms of 2-dimensional values from said extracted contour, being characterised to perform a step of determining length values and angle values. 51. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model, being characterised to perform the steps of: - determining image plane in 3D imaging space that corresponds to a detector plane, a plane coinciding with flat x-ray sensor panel or a film of a real imaging environment, where projection of a bone is formed; determining image center as a central position of a rectangular detector; - determining position of X-ray source with respect to said image plane, in imaging space; and - determining distance between object's (bone) centroid and the X-ray source, measured in the direction normal to the image plane, in the imaging space. 52. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model, being characterised to perform the steps of: - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; 

 first distance computation mechanism configured to determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; and - second distance computation mechanism configured to determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; - determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance. 53. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model, being characterised to perform the steps of: - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; - determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; - determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance; - said source object distance being determined by steps comprising further steps of: - placing a spherical ball marker with a known actual diameter near the object (bone/body) during X-ray imaging, closer to the image center, at a height from the detector plane, that is closer to the height of the object's centroid from the detector plane, by eyeballing. computing source object distance as multiplication of source film distance and the ratio of the known actual diameter of the spherical ball marker to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane; and determining diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane' as equal to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the 

 spherical ball marker measured on the final X-ray image multiplied by a digital magnification ratio, said digital magnification ratio being a ratio of the value of the distance between the positions of projections of any two points on the object's (bone) surface on the detector plane to the value of the distance between the corresponding points as measured in the final X-ray image. 54. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said camera model determination mechanism, for determining camera model, being characterised to perform the steps of: - determining position of x-ray source with respect to image centre in such a way that a normal of image plane arising from the image centre coincides the source and at a known distance from the image centre; determining source film distance as equal to the distance between an x-ray source and a detector, measured along the direction that is normal to a detector plane; - determining source object distance defined as distance between an x-ray source and a bone-centroid, which is the average position of all the surface points of a bone , measured along the direction that is normal to the image plane; - determining camera calibration as a ratio of source object distance to source film distance; said source object distance being determined by steps comprising further steps of: - placing a spherical ball marker with a known actual diameter near the object (bone/body) during X-ray imaging, closer to the image center, at a height from the detector plane, that is closer to the height of the object's centroid from the detector plane, by eyeballing. - computing source object distance as multiplication of source film distance and the ratio of the known actual diameter of the spherical ball marker to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane; and - determining diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker on the detector plane' as equal to the diameter of the circular / elliptical projection of the spherical ball marker measured on the final X-ray image multiplied by a digital magnification ratio, said digital magnification ratio being determined by a circular coin marker with known actual diameter placed on the detector while taking the X-ray image, said digital magnification ratio being approximately equal to the ratio of the known actual 

 diameter of the circular coin to diameter of the coin as visible on the final X-ray image, as measured in terms of number of pixels or mm. 55. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform the steps of: - determination of anatomical regions, said anatomical landmarks being 2-dimensional positions of unique anatomical features identified on the final X-ray image on the basis anatomical regions; and - determination of anatomical landmarks based on determined anatomical regions, said anatomical parameters being values of geometric parameters calculated on the basis of anatomical landmarks for use in 3-dimensional reconstruction. 56. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform the steps of: - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; and - determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said manual method comprising further step of specifying two points on the image that lie on the axis. 57. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform the steps of: - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; and - determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said manual method comprising further step of determining a given line along a position and orientation of the required axis. 58. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform the steps of: - dividing points of contour of a bone into subsets in such a way that the subset points correspond to distinct anatomical regions of the bone; - determining certain axes based on anatomical regions based on a manual method, said automatic method comprising further steps of: - performing geometric calculation on distinguished anatomical regions of the contour; and - determining positions of a few anatomical landmarks on the final X-ray image with respect to the extracted contours on the basis of anatomical regions. 59. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform the steps of calculating anatomical parameters on the basis of anatomical landmarks wherein parameters can be distance between two landmarks, angle between lines defined by any two landmarks, or any correlative value between landmarks. 60. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said contouring mechanism, for determining anatomical values, being characterised to perform a step of determining anatomical regions, axes, anatomical landmarks, and anatomical parameters of said pre-created 3D template model, said anatomical regions corresponding to regions distinguished in the contour of said X-ray image, said anatomical landmarks identified on the basis of anatomical regions of said template being same as the anatomical landmarks identified on the basis of anatomical regions of said contour, said anatomical parameters identified on the basis of anatomical landmarks of said template being same as the anatomical parameters identified on the basis of anatomical landmarks of the contour. 61. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said bone template model input mechanism, for 

 importing a pre-created 3-dimensional template, being characterised to perform the step of: - providing a corresponding bone template model in 3-dimensional format, said corresponding bone template model format belonging to a clinically normal bone in the form of 3D mesh with triangular elements, said bone template model being reconfigured into a shape that matches the input contours. 62. A system for obtaining a 3-dimensional image using conventional 2-dimensional x-ray images as claimed in claim 40 wherein, said pre-created 3-dimensional template is formed in the form of mesh, pre-created from CT scan of a healthy subject with average weight and height; such a data set with multiple subjects being created and wherein demographics and gender of subjects being used to discretise the data set. 63.
